title: David Attenborough: celebrating the 100th birthday of the broadcaster and naturalist
html_body:
What matters in science |
View this email in your browser Friday 8 May 2026

Hello Nature readers,
Today we celebrate the 100th birthday of naturalist David Attenborough with some of the most interesting and compelling images from his storied career.
As of 2022, there were more than 50 taxa (genera and species) named after Attenborough. (Entertainment Pictures/Alamy )

David Attenborough at 100: in photos

One of the world's best-known science communicators, David Attenborough, has presented pioneering documentaries, authored books and served as the face (and distinctive voice) of the natural world for millions of people. “His programmes have not only defined science and natural-history broadcasting, but they have also changed how we see our planet and our place within it,” says the BBC’s Jack Bootle.

Nature | Leisurely scroll
In the seminal 1979 series Life on Earth, Attenborough was approached by mountain gorillas (Gorilla beringei beringei) . (John Sparks/Nature Picture Library)

Be more Attenborough

In a 2021 speech to the United Nations COP26 climate meeting, Attenborough told delegates: “In my lifetime I’ve witnessed a terrible decline. In yours, you could and should witness a wonderful recovery.” As we mark his centenary, the most fitting legacy of the next 100 years would be for humanity to achieve that necessary recovery, argues a Nature editorial.

Nature | 8 min read

Fabricated citations are growing

An audit of 2.5 million biomedical-science papers spanning 3 years has identified thousands that contain bogus references that can’t be traced to real publications — and the rate is increasing. The findings are “conservative underestimates”, says AI researcher and study co-author Maxim Topaz. “We’re scratching the tip of the iceberg.”

Nature | 6 min read
Reference: The Lancet paper

Question of the week

This week we read that digital distractions are affecting our attention spans — but our underlying ability to focus is as strong as ever. Our feature explains that periods of mind-wandering can be beneficial, and it’s possible to set up our spaces so that we only deal with the distractions we really want. I’m personally a fan of the Pomodoro method (short periods of timed focus, interspersed with built-in breaks). But I’d like to hear how you do it — what are your preferred methods for maintaining healthy focus? Let us know at briefing@nature.com.

Features & opinion

Futures: science fiction from Nature

A chatbot achieves more than its programming in Origin story, and a superpower turns sour in Matter of taste — the latest short stories for Nature’s Futures series.

Five best science books this week

Andrew Robinson’s pick of the top five science books to read this week includes an illustrated history of the first atomic test — known as Trinity — in New Mexico, and a brutally intimate memoir by David Sussillo of how his childhood marred with violence and neglect contributed to his career as a neuroscientist.

Nature | 4 min read

Visualize whirled peas

Peas tend to be affordable and widely available — not to mention tasty and healthy, writes nutrition-education researcher and pea-vangelist Rachel Woods. She argues that the vegetable should come out of the side-dish shadows because of its high levels of protein, fibre and nutrients such as iron, and the ease with which it can be added to our diets. (Kids, if you’re reading this, I told you so.)

The Conversation | 7 min read

Anaesthetized brains can process podcasts

Researchers played podcasts to people under anaesthesia and found that a deep brain structure called the hippocampus still seemed to process language. “Much to our surprise,” neuroscientist Sameer Sheth tells the Nature Podcast, the results indicated “a very sophisticated form of processing of the natural speech that they were listening to”. Sheth and his colleagues used The Moth podcast, but he promises that “next time we will definitely use the Nature Podcast”.

Nature Podcast | 15 min listen
Subscribe to the Nature Podcast on Apple Podcasts, Spotify or YouTube Music, or use the RSS feed.
 Reference: Nature paper or read a plain(er)-language summary in the Research Briefing (Nature paywall)

Quote of the day

“Probably Tuscany — where you've got human beings and the wild world living in harmony, with mutual respect, and nature still has a bit of leeway.”

David Attenborough is probably one of the world’s most-travelled people, but in 2007 he told The Guardian that although he doesn’t mind visiting “somewhere like the Canadian wilderness for a month”, his favourite landscape is not that far from home. (18 min read)

Like Attenborough, Leif Penguinson enjoys a bit of la dolce vita. Today, our penguin friend is hiding in Italy’s Parco Naturale dei Monti Aurunci. Can you find the penguin?

The answer will be in Monday’s e-mail, all thanks to Briefing photo editor and penguin wrangler Tom Houghton.

This newsletter is always evolving — tell us what you think! Please send your feedback to briefing@nature.com.

Thanks for reading,
Flora Graham, senior editor, Nature Briefing

summary.overview: 이번 호는 생물학자이자 자연 다큐멘터리 제작자인 데이비드 애티엔버로우의 100주년을 기념하며, 그의 업적과 과학 관련 다양한 주제를 다룬 기사들을 소개합니다. 자연 보호, 과학 서적, 의학 연구, 식품 영양, 뇌 기능 등 다채로운 내용을 담고 있습니다.
covered_item_titles: 마취 상태의 뇌도 팟캐스트 처리 가능

article_briefings.title: 데이비드 애티엔버로우 100주년: 사진으로 만나다
article_briefings.briefing_ko: 100세를 맞은 데이비드 애티엔버로우의 생애와 업적을 사진으로 조명합니다. 그의 자연 다큐멘터리와 과학 서적은 수많은 사람들의 인식을 바꾼 역사적인 기여를 했습니다.

article_briefings.title: 더 나은 애티엔버로우 되기
article_briefings.briefing_ko: 애티엔버로우가 COP26에서 강조한 기후 변화 대응 메시지를 중심으로, 그의 유산이 오늘날 과학자들에게 어떤 영감을 주는지 살펴봅니다.

article_briefings.title: 가짜 참고문헌이 급증하는 이유
article_briefings.briefing_ko: 의학 논문에서 가짜 참고문헌이 증가하는 현상을 조사한 연구 결과를 소개합니다. AI가 이에 관여한 점을 비롯한 문제의 심각성에 대해 다룹니다.

article_briefings.title: 이주의 최고 과학 서적 5권
article_briefings.briefing_ko: 최근 출간된 과학 서적 중 가장 흥미로운 5권을 선정해 소개합니다. 원자 폭탄 실험과 개인의 과학적 성찰이 담긴 책이 포함되어 있습니다.

article_briefings.title: 감자 요리법, 과학적으로 설명해드립니다
article_briefings.briefing_ko: 영양학자 라achel woods가 감자의 영양 가치와 다양한 조리법을 소개하며, 식탁에서의 주요 식품으로서의 위치를 강조합니다.

article_briefings.title: 마취 상태의 뇌도 팟캐스트 처리 가능
article_briefings.briefing_ko: 마취 중인 환자의 뇌가 언어를 처리할 수 있다는 연구 결과를 보도합니다. 히포카ม프스의 역할과 관련된 신기한 실험을 다룹니다.
